title: cuscuta angulata
intro: Cuscuta angulata, also known as the angled dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants, obtaining its nutrients through a network of filaments called haustoria. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cuscuta angulata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, and support immune function. Cuscuta angulata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
